human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1130 requirements, SUD program operations boundary discipline, SAMHSA and ASAM workflow grounding, HHS Part 2 controls, scenario coverage, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and cross-industry research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for execution-processing-and-fulfillment plus cross-industry are represented and validated.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and this readiness evidence.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to SUD program operations administration, including admission readiness, consent and authorization tracking, roster and dosing workflow coordination, toxicology traceability, exception packaging, and discharge evidence control without clinical or legal drift.

Authority sources cover SAMHSA OTP certification and Part 8 obligations, SAMHSA outpatient administrative workflow, ASAM placement and service-request artifacts, and HHS Part 2 privacy controls with refresh and decay metadata.

Scenario set covers missing industry evidence, evidence-free completion attempts, authorization conflicts, privacy-sensitive redisclosure requests, clinical-boundary rejections, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant fact gaps.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; modality, state, payer, and tenant workflow differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
